mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through reduced aggregate demand and margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2010 labor market recovery was fragile with PAYEMS at 130242k
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS declines fail to predict negative 20d returns in >55% of instances, or Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 20d SPX returns remains below 0.05 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) combined with rising core inflation (CPIAUCSL acceleration) creates an implicit monetary tightening expectation that compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns as the market reprices duration risk.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3% while FEDFUNDS remains < 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% in July 2010, CPIAUCSL at 217.597
regime_hint: zero lower bound with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the interaction signal, or the joint effect of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strengthening consumer demand and corporate revenue growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices near-term earnings upside.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 5d returns in >52% of instances, or Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 5d SPX returns falls below 0.06 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction signals weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side stress that constrains corporate profitability and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions fail to predict negative 60d returns in >55% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 60d SPX returns remains below 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. When both rise together, the combined demand signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through accelerated earnings growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change > 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month change > 1%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 549k in June 2010, RSAFS at 362688m in July 2010
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with synchronized demand expansion
failure_condition: Joint HOUST and RSAFS signals fail to predict positive 20d returns in >58% of triggered instances, or the interaction Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer purchasing power and increases financial stress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 1 month
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with persistent labor market slack
failure_condition: UNRATE increases fail to predict negative 20d returns in >55% of instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE changes and 20d SPX returns remains below 0.05 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) deceleration combined with accommodative monetary policy (FEDFUNDS near zero) signals diminishing economic momentum that reduces equity risk premium and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 2.5% while FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13216.5b in Q2 2010, FEDFUNDS at 0.18%
regime_hint: weak recovery with monetary accommodation exhaustion
failure_condition: Weak GDP growth with low rates fails to predict negative 60d returns in >55% of instances, or the interaction Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) acceleration combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening productive capacity and labor demand that supports earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through improved profit margins and revenue expansion.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0.4% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change > 50k
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 92.5354 in June 2010, PAYEMS at 130242k in July 2010
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with synchronized supply and labor expansion
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO and PAYEMS signals fail to predict positive 20d returns in >58% of triggered instances, or the interaction Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
